content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Addressed all four pending amendments for planned-elsewhere reconciliation correctness. Amendments 1+3 (same root cause): Added a durably_verified HashSet<&str> in verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success that tracks which mapped_to_bead_id values were successfully persisted as verified=true in Phase 2. Phase 3 now skips comment posting for any mapping not in durably_verified, preventing duplicate comments on replay when Phase 2's record_planned_elsewhere_mapping fails but Phase 3's comment_bead succeeds. Amendment 2: Eliminated comment logic duplication by renaming verify_and_comment_mappings to verify_mappings in planned_elsewhere.rs, removing the br_mutation parameter, post_comments parameter, and all commenting code from the function body. The function now performs verification only and always returns comment_posted=false. Commenting responsibility lives solely in success_reconciliation Phase 3, removing the need for the post_comments=false workaround and the duplicated code. Amendment 4: Changed the dedup set in reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings from immutable existing_keys to mutable seen_keys, inserting each reconstructed mapping's identity key (finding_summary, mapped_to_bead_id) after it passes the dedup check. This prevents the same planned-elsewhere amendment appearing in multiple final-review aggregates from being reconstructed multiple times in the same reconciliation run.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Amendments 1+3: Added durably_verified HashSet<&str> in verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success (success_reconciliation.rs). Phase 2 inserts mapped_to_bead_id into the set only after successful record_planned_elsewhere_mapping. Phase 3 skips comment posting for any mapping not in durably_verified, ensuring comments are never posted for mappings that failed to persist.
2. [Completed] Amendment 2: Renamed verify_and_comment_mappings to verify_mappings in planned_elsewhere.rs. Removed br_mutation parameter, post_comments parameter, and all comment-posting logic from the function body. The function now returns MappingVerificationOutcome with comment_posted always false. Updated Phase 1 call site in success_reconciliation.rs to use the new signature.
3. [Completed] Amendment 4: Changed existing_keys (immutable) to seen_keys (mutable HashSet) in reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings. After each reconstructed mapping passes the dedup check, its identity key (finding_summary, mapped_to_bead_id) is inserted into seen_keys, preventing duplicate reconstructions when the same planned-elsewhere amendment appears in multiple round aggregates.
4. [Completed] Verified via grep that BrMutationAdapter import in planned_elsewhere.rs is still used by other functions (reconcile_planned_elsewhere, tests), confirming the import should not be removed despite removing it from verify_mappings.
5. [Completed] Ran cargo test (758 passed, 0 failed), cargo clippy (no new warnings from changed files), cargo fmt --check (clean).

## Validation Evidence

- cargo test: 758 passed, 0 failed across all test suites (683 lib, 174 integration, 1 unit, 9 doc-tests ignored)
- cargo fmt --check: exit 0, no formatting issues
- cargo clippy: no new warnings from success_reconciliation.rs or planned_elsewhere.rs; all 19 warnings are pre-existing in unrelated files
- Amendment 1+3 verified: durably_verified HashSet gates Phase 3 — grep confirms HashSet created at line 770, inserted at line 793 (on persist success), checked at line 805 (before comment posting)
- Amendment 2 verified: verify_mappings signature at planned_elsewhere.rs:271 takes only (br_read, active_bead_id, mappings) with no br_mutation or post_comments parameters; commenting responsibility is solely in success_reconciliation Phase 3
- Amendment 4 verified: seen_keys is mutable (line 887) with insert call at line 926 after each reconstruction, preventing cross-aggregate duplicates
- Files modified: src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs (durably_verified gating, updated Phase 1 call, mutable seen_keys with insert), src/contexts/automation_runtime/planned_elsewhere.rs (renamed to verify_mappings, removed commenting logic)

## Outstanding Risks

- Pre-existing clippy warnings (19 from code outside this change) cause cargo clippy -- -D warnings to fail; none are introduced by this change.
- The br_show_error_is_missing pattern for stale-bead detection is duplicated between cli/run.rs and planned_elsewhere.rs; a future consolidation could extract this into a shared utility on BrError.
- The per-round reconstruction in reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings scans all payload records and all final-review aggregates on every reconciliation run. For projects with many completion rounds this adds some overhead, but payload counts are bounded by completion_rounds × stages and the reconstruction short-circuits when no missing mappings are found.
- With the split verification/commenting approach, if a crash occurs after persisting verified=true but before posting the comment, the comment is permanently lost. This is an accepted tradeoff since comments are best-effort and the alternative (duplicate comments on replay) is worse.
